flashcards.back: A Java program that runs on a web server and handles client requests to generate dynamic responses.

flashcards.front: Name the three main servlet lifecycle methods.
What is the difference between doGet() and doPost()?
flashcards.back: doGet() handles HTTP GET requests (data in URL), doPost() handles HTTP POST requests (data in request body).

flashcards.front: What are the four session tracking methods?
flashcards.back: Cookies, URL rewriting, hidden form fields, and HttpSession interface.
Deployment descriptor that configures servlets, mappings, and other web application settings.
